---
title: "Flow Analysis Template"
output: html_notebook
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r}
remotes::install_local("CytoExploreR-master.zip",
                        dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages(c("ggcyto", "uwot"))
if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages("BiocManager")

BiocManager::install(c("flowCore", "flowWorkspace", "CytoML", "openCyto"))

```

```{r libraries}
library(flowCore)
library(flowWorkspace)
library(CytoML)
library(ggcyto)
library(openCyto)
library(uwot)
library(CytoExploreR)
library(gridExtra)
setwd("D:/Zhang Lab/MDS Project/srsf2_exp2/040126-SFSF2-TNFa-IL-1B-Single-Co-Culture-D0/Unmixed")
```

```{r functions}
# 1. Import FlowJo workspace directly
ws <- open_flowjo_xml("my_workspace.wsp")
gs <- flowjo_to_gatingset(ws, name = "my_group")

# 2. Or start fresh from .fcs files
files <- list.files("Unmixed/", pattern = "\\.fcs$", full.names = TRUE, recursive = TRUE)
head(files)  # verify paths look correct

fcs <- read.flowSet(files = files)
# Extract group names from the subfolder names
groups <- basename(dirname(files))
groups

pData(fcs)$group <- groups

# Check channels — Aurora will have an "Autofluorescence" channel
colnames(fcs[[1]])

# Exclude it from analysis/clustering if needed
channels_of_interest <- colnames(fcs[[1]])[!grepl("Autofluorescence|AF", 
                                                    colnames(fcs[[1]]))]
# Exclude scatter, time, and any non-fluorescence channels
linear <- c("Time", "SSC-H", "SSC-A", "FSC-H", "FSC-A", "SSC-B-H", "SSC-B-A")

fluor_channels <- colnames(fcs[[1]])[
  !colnames(fcs[[1]]) %in% linear &
  !grepl("Autofluorescence|AF", colnames(fcs[[1]]))
]

fluor_channels
# Should give you: BV421, BV605, BV785, PerCP-eFluor 710, PE, dTomato, APC, Zombie NIR, APC-Fire 750
# Look at the raw untransformed values
# fcs_trans is your transformed flowSet, but fcs is the raw original
summary(exprs(fcs[[1]])[, "Zombie NIR-A"])

# Or if you want to see all fluorescence channels at once
sapply(fluor_channels, function(ch) {
  summary(exprs(fcs[[1]])[, ch])
})
# t should be close to your data's max value, not the default 262144
# Build it as a transformerList, which is what GatingSets expect
zombie_transformer <- flowWorkspace::logicle_trans(w = 0.5, 
                                                    t = 262144, 
                                                    m = 4.5, 
                                                    a = 0)

trans_list <- transformerList("Zombie NIR-A", zombie_transformer)
gs <- transform(gs, trans_list)

# 4. Transform (Logicle, similar to FlowJo's biexponential)
# estimateLogicle handles negatives well — preferred for Aurora
trans <- estimateLogicle(fcs[[1]], channels = fluor_channels)
# Linear transform for scatter channels
linear_trans <- transformList(
  c("FSC-A", "FSC-H", "SSC-A", "SSC-H", "SSC-B-A", "SSC-B-H"),
  linearTransform(transformationId = "linear", a = 1/4000000, b = 0)
)

# Combine with your existing logicle transforms
all_trans <- c(linear_trans, trans)  # trans = your estimateLogicle object from earlier

# Apply combined transforms to the GatingSet
gs <- transform(gs, all_trans)

# 5. Gating
gs <- GatingSet(fcs_trans)
P1 <- polygonGate(filterId = "P1",
                           "FSC-A" = c(1000000, 1000000, 2000000, 4000000, 4000000),
                           "SSC-A" = c(500000, 2000000, 3000000, 3000000, 500000))
gs_pop_add(gs, P1, parent = "root")
recompute(gs)

# Check hierarchy
gs_get_pop_paths(gs)

# Add child gate (example)
P2 <- polygonGate(filterId = "P2", 
                  "FSC-A" = c(...), 
                  "FSC-H" = c(...))
gs_pop_add(gs, P2, parent = "P1")
recompute(gs)

# 6. Visualize (like FlowJo plots)
autoplot(gs[[1]], gate = "P1")
autoplot(gs[[1]])

# Plot P1 gate on FSC-A vs SSC-A
autoplot(gs[[1]], gate = "P1", bins = 256) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 5000000)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 5000000)) +
  theme_bw()

p <- ggcyto(gs[[1]], aes(x = `FSC-A`, y = `SSC-A`)) +
  geom_hex(bins = 256) +
  geom_gate("P1") +
  geom_stats("P1", type = c("count", "percent")) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = c("navy","cyan","green","yellow","red")) +
  labs(title = "P1 — Scatter gate") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

p

# 7. Get statistics
gs_pop_get_stats(gs, nodes = gs_get_pop_paths(gs))
```


```{r plot each channel as histogram}
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)

plots <- lapply(fluor_channels, function(ch) {
  dat <- as.data.frame(exprs(fcs_trans[[1]])[, ch])
  colnames(dat) <- "value"
  
  ggplot(dat, aes(x = value)) +
    geom_density(fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.5, color = NA) +
    ggtitle(ch) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 9) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 8)) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = NULL)
})

do.call(grid.arrange, c(plots, ncol = 3))
```

```{r plot each channel vs fcs}
plots <- lapply(fluor_channels, function(ch) {
  dat <- data.frame(
    fsc = exprs(fcs_trans[[1]])[, "FSC-A"],
    flu = exprs(fcs_trans[[1]])[, ch]
  )
  
  ggplot(dat, aes(x = fsc, y = flu)) +
    geom_hex(bins = 128) +
    scale_fill_gradientn(colors = c("navy","cyan","green","yellow","red")) +
    ggtitle(ch) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 9) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 8),
          legend.position = "none") +
    labs(x = "FSC-A", y = ch)
})

do.call(grid.arrange, c(plots, ncol = 3))
```

```{r retransform problematic channels}
problem_channels <- c("Zombie NIR-A", "dTomato-A", "BV785-A")
sapply(problem_channels, function(ch) {
  quantile(exprs(fcs[[1]])[, ch], c(0.01, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.99))
})

# Auto-estimate everything except the problem channels
fluor_auto <- fluor_channels[!fluor_channels %in% problem_channels]
trans_auto <- estimateLogicle(fcs[[1]], channels = fluor_auto)

trans_manual <- transformList(
  c("Zombie NIR-A", "dTomato-A", "BV785-A"),
  list(
    logicleTransform(w = 0.5, t = 262144,   m = 4.5, a = 0),  # Zombie NIR
    logicleTransform(w = 1.0, t = 262144,   m = 4.5, a = 0),  # dTomato — wide w for negatives
    logicleTransform(w = 0.5, t = 4000000,  m = 4.5, a = 0)   # BV785 — t raised to cover 99th pct
  )
)

fcs_trans <- transform(fcs, trans_auto)
fcs_trans <- transform(fcs_trans, trans_manual)
fcs_trans <- transform(fcs_trans, linear_trans)

gs <- GatingSet(fcs_trans)
gs_pop_add(gs, P1, parent = "root")
recompute(gs)
```


```{r}
# Get all fcs files with their folder paths
fcs_files <- list.files("data/", pattern = "\\.fcs$", 
                         full.names = TRUE, recursive = TRUE)

# Extract group name from folder name
groups <- basename(dirname(fcs_files))

# Load all files
cs <- load_cytoset_from_fcs(fcs_files)

# Assign group metadata
pData(cs)$group <- groups
pData(cs)
#>       name     group
#> 1  sample1      PBMC
#> 2  sample2      PBMC
#> 3  sample3     Tumor
#> 4  sample4     Tumor
#> 5  sample5        LN
#> 6  sample6        LN
```

```{r}
# Wrap your flowSet in a CytoExploreR GatingSet
gs <- cyto_setup(fcs_trans, gatingTemplate = "my_gates.csv")

# Open interactive gating window — click to draw polygon, right-click to close
cyto_gate_draw(gs,
               parent    = "root",
               alias     = "P1",
               channels  = c("FSC-A", "SSC-A"),
               gatingTemplate = "my_gates.csv")
# Singlets
cyto_gate_draw(gs,
               parent   = "P1",
               alias    = "Singlets",
               channels = c("FSC-A", "FSC-H"),
               gatingTemplate = "my_gates.csv")

# Live cells (after Zombie NIR)
cyto_gate_draw(gs,
               parent         = "Singlets",
               alias          = "Live",
               channels       = c("FSC-A", "Zombie NIR-A"),
               gatingTemplate = "my_gates.csv",
               xlim           = c(0, 4500000),
               ylim           = c(0, 1.5))



# And so on for each population...
# Gates are auto-saved to "my_gates.csv" — reload next session with:
gs <- cyto_setup(fcs_trans, gatingTemplate = "my_gates.csv")
cyto_gatingTemplate_apply(gs, "my_gates.csv")

cyto_stats_compute(gs,
                   alias = c("P1", "Singlets", "Live"),
                   stat  = "percent")
```

```{r gate mpp3 into quadrants}
mpp3work <- FetchData(mpp3_filtered, vars = c("adt_ESAM", "rna_ENSMUSG00000030336"))
mpp3_filtered <- subset(mpp3, adt_ESAM > 0 & rna_ENSMUSG00000030336 > 0)
Idents(mpp3) <- "gate3"
FeatureScatter(mpp3, feature1 = "adt_ESAM", feature2 = "rna_ENSMUSG00000030336") + 
  scale_x_flowjo_biexp(pos = 5) + 
  scale_y_flowjo_biexp(widthBasis = -150,
                       pos = 3) 

adtscatter <- FeatureScatter(mpp3_filtered, feature1 = "adt_ESAM", feature2 = "rna_ENSMUSG00000030336", group.by = "gate5") + 
  scale_x_flowjo_biexp(pos = 5) + 
  scale_y_flowjo_biexp(pos = 8,
                       neg = 2,
                       widthBasis = -500)+ 
  geom_density_2d(bins = 15) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = cd27_cutoff, linetype = "solid", color = "black", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = esam_cutoff, linetype = "solid", color = "black", linewidth = 1) + 
  labs(title = "MPP3 ESAM vs CD27", x = "ESAM (ADT)", y = "CD27 (RNA)")
adtscatter

ggsave("mpp3esamcd27gate.png", adtscatter, dpi = 600)

summary(mpp3work$adt_ESAM)
summary (mpp3work$rna_ENSMUSG00000030336)
hist(log10(mpp3work$adt_ESAM), breaks = 100)           # find the ESAM x-gate valley
hist(mpp3work$rna_ENSMUSG00000030336, breaks = 100)  # find the CD27 y-gate valley

esam_cutoff <- quantile(mpp3work$adt_ESAM, 0.25)  
cd27_cutoff <- quantile(mpp3work$rna_ENSMUSG00000030336, 0.25)

mpp3_filtered$gate5 <- NULL
mpp3_filtered$gate5 <- ifelse(mpp3work$adt_ESAM > esam_cutoff & mpp3work$rna_ENSMUSG00000030336 < cd27_cutoff, "E+", 
                         ifelse(mpp3work$adt_ESAM < esam_cutoff & mpp3work$rna_ENSMUSG00000030336 < cd27_cutoff, "DN",
                                ifelse(mpp3work$adt_ESAM > esam_cutoff & mpp3work$rna_ENSMUSG00000030336 > cd27_cutoff, "DP", 
                                       ifelse(mpp3work$adt_ESAM < esam_cutoff & mpp3work$rna_ENSMUSG00000030336 > cd27_cutoff, "27+", NA))))
table(mpp3_filtered$gate5)

FeatureScatter(mpp3_filtered, feature1 = "adt_ESAM", feature2 = "rna_ENSMUSG00000030336", group.by = "gate5") + 
  scale_x_flowjo_biexp(pos = 5) + 
  scale_y_flowjo_biexp(pos = 8,
                       neg = 2,
                       widthBasis = -500)+ 
  geom_density_2d(bins = 15)
```
